author: Susan "Lady D" Damon
release_boundary: Line-edited review batch. This is not final KDP upload approval.

source_files.library_source: /Users/IDC2.5/Documents/LADY D/Production Library/01 Surrendering to God's Love/01 Manuscript/Month 01 - January/Days 022-028 Manuscript.md

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Yield to the Heart That Calls You into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Let glad trust settle into your body today; the Father is holding more than your thoughts.
edits.reason: Connects the Psalm 16 rest-security theme to embodied anxiety without sounding generic.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Anchor Love That Sends You into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Do the quiet faithful thing in front of you; God can carry its fruit farther than you can see.
edits.reason: Keeps the Ruth/Obed generational-faithfulness theme practical and memorable.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Discover Covenant Mercy into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Receive mercy, then rise with it; the Father's forgiveness still has a future for you.
edits.reason: Preserves mercy that restores and calls forward from Deuteronomy 10:11.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Receive the Father's Welcome into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Let conviction become welcome today; the Father is softening what fear taught you to guard.
edits.reason: Keeps heart-surrender conviction tender rather than condemnatory.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Trust Beloved Identity into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Remember how mercy found you, then make room for someone who feels outside.
edits.reason: Turns beloved identity into concrete welcome for the stranger.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Let Hope Rise Love That Finds You into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Bless the small beginning in your hands; the Father's love knows how to grow what He plants.
edits.reason: Matches the few-to-stars promise without inflating the reader's role.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Carry Grace Before Striving into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Return before you perform today; let obedience begin where the Father's grace calls you home.
edits.reason: Preserves the grace-before-performance and Sabbath-as-return guardrails.

judge_auditor_loops.evidence: All seven impact lines now vary by entry theme and avoid the repeated `Let the Father's love carry` frame.
Sabbath remains seventh-day/Saturday; commandment-keeping and obedience stay framed as response to grace.
The batch file now has zero old Volume 1 morning-impact templates.
Repo source, public mirror, and Production Library manuscript receive the same edited text.

next_loop: Continue author-voice line edits in seven-day batches, then regenerate masters/interiors and update the review site after each meaningful gate.
